landing.hero.headline: Put AI agents on your chip team
landing.hero.subheadline: From pin-to-pin cross-referencing to a 24/7 AI FAE on your website — Barbot turns semiconductor workflows into AI agents. One platform, one credit balance, every product.
landing.features.title: Built for hardware engineers
landing.features.description: Everything you need to qualify a substitute part — from catalog search to AI datasheet analysis.
landing.features.credits.title: Credit-based billing
landing.features.credits.description: Pay per analysis with credits. Cache hits are free, failed runs are never charged.
landing.pricing.title: Pricing
landing.pricing.description: Choose the plan that works for you.
landing.faq.title: Frequently asked questions
landing.faq.description: Everything you might want to know before your first comparison.
landing.cta.headline: Give your chip business an AI agent team
landing.cta.subheadline: Start with one product or the whole platform — one sign-up, one credit balance.
landing.footer.tagline: AI agents for the semiconductor industry.

settings.wiki.title: Context Wiki Assistant
settings.wiki.no_summary: No summary is available for this context yet.
settings.wiki.context_description: Select one or more wiki sources on the left. The assistant panel will package them into context.md before asking the agent, so answers stay focused on the material you chose.
settings.wiki.start_description: Choose the materials you want the agent to focus on, then ask a question from this panel.
settings.wiki.no_selection: Select an area on the diagram
settings.wiki.quick_why: Why is it designed this way?
settings.wiki.feynman_start_description: Zoom into a teardown image, select a component or circuit area, then ask the coach what it is and why it was designed that way.
settings.ai_support.title: AI Support Command Center
settings.ai_support.description: A first implementation pass from the SiteGPT research PRD: chatbot launch readiness, knowledge sources, leads, human support, agent permissions, usage, billing, and audit controls in one operator surface.
settings.ai_support.metric_chatbots_desc: One default workspace bot ready for installation.
settings.ai_support.metric_knowledge_des: FAQ, text, links, and files tracked as source types.
settings.ai_support.metric_handoffs_desc: Escalations are enabled but notification routing is pending.
settings.ai_support.metric_agent_actions: Draft, classify, audit, and health-check tasks.
settings.ai_support.readiness_descriptio: The PRD's P0 scope condensed into a working operator checklist.
settings.ai_support.guardrail_scope_desc: Agents are bound to chatbot, source type, and action policy.
settings.ai_support.guardrail_approval_d: Publish, delete, external send, billing, and member changes require human approval.
settings.ai_support.guardrail_rollback_d: Prompts, personas, custom responses, and human support copy need versions.
settings.ai_support.launch_description: The minimum operational path before putting the chatbot on a real site.
settings.ai_support.launch_install_desc: Embed code, SDK guidance, and live installation checks are ready.
settings.ai_support.launch_knowledge_des: Custom responses, text snippets, links, and files are represented in the admin model.
settings.ai_support.launch_human_desc: Escalation copy, routing settings, email, and webhook delivery are connected.
settings.ai_support.launch_agent_desc: Agents can draft and classify by default; publishing remains gated by approval.
settings.ai_support.launch_production_de: Backups, alerting, retention, rate limits, and domain restrictions must be configured.
settings.ai_support.launch_security_desc: PII masking, token rotation, and destructive-action confirmation are required before production.
settings.ai_support.knowledge_descriptio: PRD source types mapped into a single operational queue.
settings.ai_support.agent_description: A least-privilege matrix for Codex, Hermes, CLI tokens, and MCP connections.
settings.ai_support.agent_token_desc: Tokens should expire, rotate, and show last-used metadata.
settings.ai_support.agent_diff_desc: Agents preview prompt, persona, and knowledge edits before publishing.
settings.ai_support.agent_mcp_desc: Expose machine-readable tools while keeping write scopes explicit.
settings.ai_support.feature_install_desc: JavaScript, iframe, inline container, SDK methods, and install verification.
settings.ai_support.feature_chat_desc: Search conversations, inspect transcripts, feedback, and knowledge gaps.
settings.ai_support.feature_leads_desc: Collect email, name, phone, source page, owner, and lifecycle status.
settings.ai_support.feature_human_desc: Escalation buttons, confirmation copy, notifications, and assignment.
settings.ai_support.feature_appearance_d: Name, tooltip, welcome message, placeholder, colors, and preview.
settings.ai_support.feature_integrations: Slack, Zendesk, Freshdesk, Messenger, Google Chat, and webhooks.
settings.ai_support.feature_billing_desc: Plan, trial, payment method, addons, invoice history, and cancellation.
settings.ai_support.feature_usage_desc: Chatbots, pages, members, models, reset date, and CSV export.
settings.ai_support.feature_audit_desc: Every human and agent action should leave an actor, diff, and result trail.
settings.ai_support.ops_chatbots_desc: Create installable bots, activate them, and copy the public bootstrap snippet.
settings.ai_support.ops_create_chatbot_d: Create the operational container for one site, product, or support surface.
settings.ai_support.ops_no_chatbots: No chatbots yet. Create one before adding knowledge or agent tokens.
settings.ai_support.ops_install_help: Paste a public page URL after embedding the widget to verify installation.
settings.ai_support.ops_knowledge_desc: Add the first P0 knowledge entries for FAQ, snippets, links, and files.
settings.ai_support.ops_add_source_desc: Create a draft source that an admin or agent can review and publish.
settings.ai_support.ops_sync_jobs_desc: Review knowledge ingestion status and retry failed source syncs before launch.
settings.ai_support.ops_no_sync_jobs: No sync jobs yet. Add a knowledge source first.
settings.ai_support.ops_confirm_sync_des: The source status and sync metadata will be updated, and failed sources may be retried.
settings.ai_support.ops_tokens_desc: Create scoped tokens for Codex, Hermes, CLI, or MCP workflows.
settings.ai_support.ops_create_token_des: The raw token is shown once and copied to your clipboard on creation.
settings.ai_support.ops_human_settings_d: Configure escalation buttons, customer-facing copy, and notification routing.
settings.ai_support.ops_human_settings_e: Create a chatbot before configuring human support.
settings.ai_support.ops_appearance_desc: Control the public widget name, welcome copy, input hint, launcher label, and brand color.
settings.ai_support.ops_launch_ops_desc: Track the operational controls required before the chatbot is installed on a real production site.
settings.ai_support.ops_prompt_persona_d: Version the chatbot instructions and support persona directly from the admin console.
settings.ai_support.ops_confirm_revoke_t: The agent will lose API access immediately. This cannot be undone.
settings.ai_support.ops_confirm_agent_ap: The proposed config will be published and included in the version history.
settings.ai_support.ops_confirm_agent_re: The proposal will be closed without publishing its config changes.
settings.ai_support.ops_confirm_rollback: A new published version will be created from the selected historical content.
settings.ai_support.ops_confirm_retry_no: Email or webhook delivery may contact external systems again.
settings.ai_support.ops_leads_desc: Recent captured contacts and qualification status.
settings.ai_support.ops_escalations_desc: Open escalation requests that need assignment or closure.
settings.ai_support.ops_conversations_de: Recent widget conversations with contact, source page, status, and message count.
settings.ai_support.ops_transcript_desc: Inspect user and assistant messages, including cited knowledge sources.
settings.ai_support.ops_select_conversat: Select a conversation to view the transcript.
settings.ai_support.ops_audit_desc: Recent human and agent actions with actor, resource, approval, and result status.
settings.ai_support.ops_agent_runs_desc: Review proposed prompt, persona, and support-copy changes before they publish.
settings.ai_support.ops_create_agent_run: Create the same pending approval record an external agent would submit.
settings.ai_support.ops_versions_desc: Published prompt and support-copy history, with rollback controls.
settings.ai_support.usage_desc: Operational usage by chatbot for monthly review, billing checks, and agent health reports.
settings.ai_support.usage_empty: No usage data yet. Create a chatbot and send a widget conversation first.
settings.ai_support.next_description: The current page is the operational shell; the next step is wiring persistent modules and APIs.
settings.ai_support.next_data_desc: Create chatbot, knowledge source, lead, escalation, agent token, and audit tables.
settings.ai_support.next_api_desc: Add typed routes for chatbots, knowledge, leads, agent policies, and audit logs.
settings.ai_support.next_widget_desc: Connect installation, public widget settings, chat runtime, and escalation events.

chips.search.subtitle: Search chips by part number, manufacturer, or description
chips.search.compare_hint: Pick chips on their detail pages, or upload datasheets on the Compare page

compare.form.subtitle: Pick 2–10 chips from the catalog or upload datasheet PDFs; AI produces a parameter-level comparison with page-anchored provenance
compare.form.upload_hint: PDF only, up to 10 files, 50MB each. Text is extracted on upload.
compare.form.prompt_placeholder: e.g. focus on power consumption differences
compare.form.cost_hint: Each run costs {credits} credits (cache hits are free)
compare.report.cache_hit: Served from cache — no credits charged
compare.trace.empty: No traces extracted for this run
settings.compare_history.title: Compare History
settings.compare_history.delete_confirm: This removes the record from your history. Continue?
admin.chips.title: Chips
admin.chips.delete_confirm: This deletes the chip and its substitutes/BOM rows. Continue?
admin.chips.import_hint: Columns: part_number (required), manufacturer, description, sheet_url, parameter
admin.chip_segments.title: Chip Segments
admin.chip_segments.delete_confirm: Child segments are deleted and chips detached. Continue?
admin.chip_compare.title: Compare Runs
admin.chip_compare.preheat_hint: Runs the pipeline without charging credits; results serve future cache hits. Chips must exist in the catalog with datasheet URLs.
landing.features.catalog.title: Chip catalog & search
landing.features.catalog.description: Exact and fuzzy search over part numbers, manufacturers, and descriptions, organized by segment taxonomy.
landing.features.pin2pin.title: Pin-to-pin substitute DB
landing.features.pin2pin.description: Curated pin-compatible alternative mappings — see domestic and cross-vendor replacements at a glance.
landing.features.ai_compare.title: AI datasheet comparison
landing.features.ai_compare.description: Compare 2–10 chips in one run. The report streams live: executive summary, parameter table, differences, and design-change checklist.
landing.features.provenance.title: Page-anchored provenance
landing.features.provenance.description: Every extracted parameter cites the datasheet page it came from, with a four-level difference rating you can audit.
landing.features.fast_parse.title: Instant PDF parsing
landing.features.fast_parse.description: Datasheets are text-extracted the moment you upload them — no queues, no external OCR round-trips, cached by file hash.
landing.how.title: How it works
landing.how.description: Three steps from part number to substitution decision.
landing.how.step1.title: Pick your chips
landing.how.step1.description: Search the catalog or drop in datasheet PDFs — up to 10 chips per run.
landing.how.step2.title: AI reads the datasheets
landing.how.step2.description: Every page is parsed and analyzed; the comparison streams to your screen in real time.
landing.how.step3.title: Audit and export
landing.how.step3.description: Review the parameter traces with page citations, add notes, and export as Markdown or CSV.
landing.faq.accuracy.question: How accurate are the AI outputs?
landing.faq.accuracy.answer: Chip P2P cites the datasheet page for every extracted parameter with a four-level difference rating; AI FAE answers only from your approved knowledge sources and says so when it doesn't know.
landing.faq.sources.question: Where does the data come from?
landing.faq.sources.answer: Chip P2P reads the datasheets you upload or the catalog's official datasheet URLs. AI FAE reads the docs, links, and snippets you connect. Nothing is invented.
landing.faq.cost.question: How does billing work?
landing.faq.cost.answer: One credit balance covers every product. A comparison run costs 10 credits (cache hits free); an AI FAE generated answer costs 1 credit. Credits never expire silently — see pricing for plans.
landing.faq.formats.question: What can I upload?
landing.faq.formats.answer: Chip P2P: text-based PDF datasheets, up to 10 files of 50MB each per run. AI FAE: web links, files, text snippets, and custom responses.
landing.faq.languages.question: What languages are supported?
landing.faq.languages.answer: The platform ships in English and Chinese. Chip P2P reports can be generated in 8 languages; AI FAE replies in the language your customer writes in.
compare.edit.publish_hint: Publishes the current report (including your edits) as a blog article.
compare.chat.title: Chip Assistant
compare.chat.empty_hint: Ask anything about the catalog or this comparison — answers are grounded in datasheet traces.
landing.products.title: One platform, a growing agent team
landing.products.description: Purpose-built AI agents for the semiconductor industry — all billed from a single credit balance.
landing.products.chip_p2p.name: Chip P2P
landing.products.chip_p2p.tagline: AI reads the datasheets and finds pin-to-pin alternatives in minutes — parameter-level diffs with page-anchored provenance and one-click reports.
landing.products.ai_fae.name: AI FAE
landing.products.ai_fae.tagline: A 24/7 AI Field Application Engineer for your website — answers technical questions, suggests parts, captures leads, hands off tickets.
landing.products.coming.name: More agents coming
landing.products.coming.tagline: BOM risk scanning, inventory matching, compliance checks — the next agents for your chip team are on the way.
landing.credits.title: One credit balance, every product
landing.credits.description: Pay for what you use: a comparison run costs 10 credits, an AI FAE answer costs 1 credit, cache hits are free. Top up once, spend anywhere on the platform.
products.chip_p2p.title: Chip P2P — AI pin-to-pin alternative analysis
products.chip_p2p.description: Upload datasheets or pick chips from the catalog; AI produces an engineering-grade comparison with page-anchored provenance for every parameter.
products.ai_fae.title: AI FAE — your website's application engineer
products.ai_fae.description: Train it on your docs and product pages. It answers technical questions, recommends parts, captures leads, and escalates to humans when needed.
console.hub.title: Barbot Console
console.hub.description: Your AI agent team — pick a product to work in
chat.title: Chip QA Chat
chat.subtitle: Persistent Q&A sessions with the chip pin-to-pin assistant, grounded in the catalog and your comparison reports.
chat.delete_confirm_desc: The session and all of its messages will be removed.
chat.empty_sessions: No sessions yet — create one to start asking.
chat.select_hint: Select a session on the left, or create a new one.
chat.empty_thread: Ask about chips, substitutes, or one of your comparison reports.
settings.device.title: Device authorization
settings.device.description: An agent or CLI tool is requesting access to your Barbot account.
settings.device.approved: Authorized — return to your terminal to continue.
settings.device.signin_required: Please sign in first to authorize this device.
settings.device.warning: Only authorize if you started this request yourself. The device will receive an API key that can spend your credits.
console.desktop_auth.title: Authorize Harvey
console.desktop_auth.description: The Harvey agent workbench is requesting access to your Barbot account.
console.desktop_auth.signin_required: Please sign in to your Barbot account first.
console.desktop_auth.invalid_callback: Invalid callback address — start the login from the app again.
console.desktop_auth.warning: The app will chat and run tasks as you, and usage will consume your credits. Only authorize if you started this login yourself.
compare.edit.empty_hint: Run an analysis first — the finished report becomes editable here.
compare.empty.title: Three steps to a client-ready comparison
compare.empty.step1_desc: Search the catalog by part number, or upload datasheet PDFs — 2 to 10 chips.
compare.empty.step2_desc: AI compares parameter by parameter with datasheet page provenance. Cache hits are free.
compare.empty.step3_desc: Edit the report, export MD/CSV, publish to the blog, or keep asking in Q&A.
compare.diagram.desc_placeholder: Describe the system, e.g. a 16-cell BMS master based on STM32F103 with an ADBMS6816 AFE, isoSPI daisy chain, CAN to the vehicle bus…
compare.diagram.empty_hint: Describe the system above — Structured SVG gives exact labels and a downloadable vector; AI Rendered produces a datasheet-style illustration.
compare.diagram.page_subtitle: Describe an electronic system and get a functional block diagram — exact-label structured SVG, or a datasheet-style AI rendering.
compare.diagram.example_bms_text: A 16-cell battery management system master board based on an STM32F103C8T6 MCU and an ADBMS6816 analog front-end, isoSPI daisy chain to the AFE, CAN bus to the vehicle, 12V input regulated to 3.3V by an LDO.
compare.diagram.example_mcu_text: An STM32F103C8T6 minimal system board: 8 MHz crystal, SWD debug header, USB device port, reset circuit, boot mode jumpers, powered from USB 5V through an AMS1117-3.3 LDO.
compare.diagram.example_motor_text: A BLDC motor controller: STM32 MCU generating 6-channel PWM into a three-phase gate driver and MOSFET half-bridges, hall sensor feedback, DC bus current sensing via shunt + op-amp, CAN communication, 24V input with buck converter to 5V and LDO to 3.3V.
compare.diagram.example_iot_text: A battery-powered wireless sensor node: ESP32 MCU, SHT40 temperature/humidity sensor on I2C, SX1278 LoRa radio on SPI, lithium battery with TP4056 charger and battery gauge, solar input.
compare.diagram.sample_svg_caption: Structured SVG — exact labels, downloadable vector
compare.diagram.example_coffee_text: The functional flow of an automatic coffee machine (like a Jura): bean hopper, grinder, dosing scale, water tank, pump, boiler/thermoblock, brew unit, milk system, waste bin — show the water path and the coffee path with clear stage-by-stage flow.
compare.diagram.example_bio_text: Cellular respiration at a glance for students: how glucose turns into energy inside a cell — glycolysis, the Krebs cycle, and the electron transport chain, with the main molecules labeled on the arrows: glucose, pyruvate, ATP, NADH, FADH2, CO2, O2, H2O.
